job.title: Graduate Research Assistant
description: Conduct research on controllable text generation using large language models and causal inference
Develop methods for counterfactual text generation and style transfer using LoRA, contrastive learning, and representation-level constraints
Design evaluation frameworks using LLM-as-a-judge and embedding-based semantic metrics
Publish research on robust NLP models and adversarial detection in venues such as AACL-IJCNLP and Entropy

job.title: Graduate Teaching Assistant
description: Assist in teaching undergraduate computer science courses including machine learning and data structures
Lead lab sessions and mentor students on programming assignments and research projects
Evaluate assignments and support course logistics for large classes

extracurricular.title: President
extracurricular.location: University of Arkansas, Fayetteville, AR, USA
description: Served as President of the Nepalese Student Association (NeSA) for the 2024–2025 academic year
Organized major cultural and community events including Nepali New Year Night, Nepali Game Night, and Deusi Bhailo celebration
Managed organizational funds, budgeting, and financial planning for student events
Coordinated with university administration and local Nepalese community organizations
Led event planning, logistics, volunteer coordination, and community outreach

other_info: Research focus: Large Language Models, Generative AI, Robust NLP, Causal Inference

education.degree: B.Sc. in Computer Science and Information Technology (BSc CSIT)
